keys.meta_description: Model steps, branches, and owners—publish workflows without code.
keys.hero_description: Drag, branch, and publish without code.
keys.features_0_description: Visual canvas for steps and ownership.
keys.features_2_description: History with who acted and when.
keys.sections_0_body: Inboxes and DMs hide delays and history.
keys.sections_1_body: The canvas is the system of record across modules.
keys.sections_2_body: Automation carries branches; escalations stay visible.
keys.sections_3_body: Processes new hires can follow without shadow rules.
keys.faq_0_q: Do I need technical skills to build a workflow?
keys.faq_0_a: No. The visual canvas uses drag-and-drop steps, branch conditions written in plain language, and assignment rules based on roles or org structure—no code required.
keys.faq_1_q: Can workflows trigger across different modules?
keys.faq_1_a: Yes. A workflow can span HR, finance, and operations. For example: expense approval → accounting post → manager notification in a single connected flow.
keys.faq_2_q: What happens if a workflow step is missed or stalls?
keys.faq_2_a: Escalation rules can auto-assign to a fallback approver or notify a manager after a configurable timeout. Every stall is logged with the timestamp and context.
keys.faq_3_q: Can we start small and expand?
keys.faq_3_a: Yes. Turn on modules as you grow—HR, finance, analytics, and workflow blocks scale together.
keys.faq_4_a: Use the Pricing page for tiers; Enterprise adds SLA, scale, and governance options.
keys.faq_5_q: How do I get a demo?
keys.faq_5_a: Use Talk to sales or Book demo from the header—our team can align Automation and Scheduler to your process.
keys.faq_6_a: Contact our team for a walkthrough tailored to your workflow.
keys.faq_7_a: Contact our team for a walkthrough tailored to your workflow.
